intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called "Peyrousia bracteata." However, I found a plant called "Pueraria bracteata" or "Pueraria montana var. bracteata", also known as the "Pueraria plant" or "Kudzu vine". 

Here is a paragraph about Pueraria bracteata:

Pueraria bracteata is a perennial vine native to East Asia, belonging to the legume family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in lowering blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and reducing blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Pueraria bracteata include isoflavones, such as daidzein and genistein, as well as other compounds like furostanol saponins and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Pueraria bracteata include tea, capsules, and tablets, as well as traditional Chinese medicinal formulas, such as Pueraria lobata and Pueraria thunbergiana.
